We contacted Finn Law Firm with the intention of pursuing a malpractice suit on behalf of our mom.
It was concluded, and agreed upon, that we would not move forward with a suit.
Although we did not move forward with a case, our family would like to extend our gratitude to the Finn Law Firm. We were treated with empathy, patience, and respect as we navigated through a painful and very emotional experience. We felt understood and supported throughout our journey with Finn Law Firm. We were impressed with the medical knowledge of Mr. Finn and his commitment to research.
In addition, efforts in regard to communication were exemplary.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Beth,

Thank you very much for sharing your experience with Finn Law Firm, even though you decided not to pursue a lawsuit. We understand how challenging and emotional these decisions can be, especially when it involves the well-being of a loved one. Our priority is always to provide clear, compassionate, and informed guidance to our clients, regardless of the outcome. We are deeply grateful for your recognition of our efforts in communication and our commitment to thorough research and understanding. Should you ever need assistance again, or have any further questions, please do not hesitate to reach out to us. Your family has our utmost respect and best wishes.

Warm regards,
The Finn Law Firm Team

reviews.text: I chose The Finn Law Firm to take care of my car accident lawsuit and it was one of the best decisions I ever made. I originally started out with a different law firm and immediately wished I had done more research on which law firm to choose. The original firm I chose was very poor at communicating with me and was pushing me in different directions that I did not want to go with trying to fix my back injury to better my case. I then switched over to Larry Finn, who was very personable, friendly, and an advocate for me receiving the proper care for my injured back. He was always keeping me informed on things and answered any questions I had making sure I understood things completely.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Nick,

Thank you so much for your review and for trusting The Finn Law Firm with your car accident lawsuit. We're thrilled to hear that switching to our firm was a beneficial decision for you. Communication, clarity, and genuine care for our clients' health and legal rights are cornerstones of our practice. We are particularly pleased to know that Larry's efforts made a significant difference in your case and recovery. We always strive to ensure our clients are fully informed and comfortable with the progress of their cases. If you have any more questions or need further assistance in the future, do not hesitate to reach out.

Warm regards,
The Finn Law Firm Team

asked him about something i had been thinking about for months. i went in because i had noticed the past couple times i visited social security the sign in the hallway showing services. because of the availability & my lack of knowledge of where lawyers are (even though i ask around, ask social workers etc) Andrew A. Barone basically helped me out a lot via a free consultation. in just 15 minutes he helped me save time on doing further paperwork. i am grateful. thank you so much!!!

notes on availability:
- he's on the first floor of the building so that helps out. i don't use a wheelchair, but i think you might be able to use one.
- social security also opens up at 9AM (& it's on the 6th floor. there are elevators) so i guess if you wanted to go to social security in the same day, you should probably figure that into your schedule. it might be only a few minutes for a consultation, but the line/queue at the social security office might be a concern (in a similar way to public transit).
- i don't know his pricing or at what point it stops being a consultation, so you'll have to ask him.
- i kind of wanted to say this office has a convenient location, but because i'm disabled (& hello "concentration in disability") i think "available" & "accessible" are better fitting for this situation.

this place is awesome & i hope this office stays around for a long time!

azdominguez@azdlawfirm.com

reviews.ownerResponse:
Congratulations to your father on receiving his lawful permanent residency. It is our goal to get an approval on all of our cases. Thank you for your taking the time to post a review. We take all feedback seriously and use the information to better our services. One of the frustrating aspects of going through the process with immigration is that the service (USCIS) requests new originals at different stages of the process. Given your experience we will change our process to advise clients to get multiple sets of originals at one time to avoid multiple trips.
I would like to apologize for doing anything that made your dad feel unwelcome or undervalued as a client. Since we are 100% a referral only boutique immigration law firm, how we treat our clients is an important part of our client experience. We strive to do better day after day and we will take your feedback and use that information to provide better service to future clients.

reviews.text:
After my dad passed away in April 2024, my mom and I filed for Dependency and Indemnity Compensation (DIC) through the VA. Despite working with a VA liaison and following up for 10 months, we received no real updates—just that the claim was still pending. Then, my mom received a notice from the VA, which she mistakenly believed was a denial of the DIC claim. Unfortunately, even the local VA liaison misinterpreted it, causing a lot of confusion. Given that my dad’s passing was clearly linked to his disability, we realized we needed legal representation and turned to James Comerford.

From our first conversation, James took the time to listen, understand our situation, and guide us through the process. Initially, we thought we needed to file a rebuttal, but after investigating, he discovered the VA had actually misinformed us—the claim was still being processed. He assured us he would follow up, and shortly after, he called with the great news: the claim had been approved. Life-Changing Representation – Twenty years ago, I suffered a significant spinal injury and severe mental health damage from specialized military training. For years, I was deceived into believing I wasn’t eligible for benefits. Despite clear medical records showing my on-duty injuries, I was repeatedly stonewalled by bureaucratic processes and a legal framework designed to deny, deny, deny.

By mid-2024, I faced yet another denial. Frustrated but determined, I started reaching out to attorneys. I spoke to a lot of them. For me, the right vibe and connection are essential—if the relationship doesn’t feel right, I won’t move forward. After all, attorneys get a portion of the back pay, so the decision has to feel good both professionally and personally.

I passed on several other attorneys and ultimately chose Jim Comerford to represent me. And brothers and sisters, my life has been forever changed. Jim took me from a measly 10% for tinnitus to 80% and finally to 100% disability in an impressively short amount of time.

The best part? Hearing Jim’s personal passion for my case. As veterans, we’re used to feeling like “just another number,” but with Jim, it’s different. His heart is truly focused on you. You’ll feel it. You’ll see it.

If you’re tired of being played by “the system,” do yourself a favor and tag Jim Comerford into the ring. He’ll fight for you like you deserve.

Thank you, Jim, for giving me my life back.

Jim has been representing me through my Social Security Disability adventure for 6 years.  I knew my case would not be easy because of my disabilities. I contacted Jim after I initially applied for SSDI.  My case was denied the first time through.  Unfortunately, the Rheumatologist I had been seeing for 17 years kept horrible records, barely recording any medical history.  After reviewing them, Jim knew this was going to be an uphill battle, but battle on he did.  Jim filed an appeal on my behalf and I finally had my day in court.  Fibro fog, memory issues and pain are difficult cases to present, but Jim did a great job.  I was awarded a partially favorable decision, meaning I would receive SSDI payments going forward. However, I did not agree with the date of disability onset determined by the judge.  So, Jim filed an appeal on my behalf to amend the judge's decision of date of  disability onset.  It took two years, but I did have my second day in court.  Jim presented my case in a passionate and professional way.  He has gone above and beyond what I believe a lot of attorneys would have done.  Through all six years, Jim has always been accessible, returned e-mails and phone calls in a timely manner.  He communicated with me every step of the way.  He's been patient and kind, listening to my concerns and calming my fears.  Jim has kept a positive and upbeat attitude through this process.  He's given me fantastic representation and I couldn't have asked for a better attorney to be with me on this journey.  I'd highly recommend Jim to anybody who's applying for SSDI.  He's got the knowledge, experience and patience to navigate the extremely complicated and muddy waters of the Social Security Disability system.

That’s when a close friend of mine recommended Michael Ibrahim. Michael worked tirelessly, even outside of normal hours, on my mom’s case. He kept us informed every step of the way, and he dedicated a lot of time to understanding our family’s specific needs. He personally set up meetings with my mom, making sure to communicate with her directly in Spanish, which was incredibly important for her to fully grasp what was happening.
My mom had nothing but good things to say about Michael. She described him as being kind, but also very honest, he didn’t sugarcoat the situation, but he reassured her and let her know he would do everything in his power to help her. It was clear that he was more than just a lawyer in this case; he genuinely cared about my mom’s well being.
After months of hard work, Michael somehow managed to do the impossible. Despite the challenges and obstacles in our way, he was able to secure my mom’s release from ICE custody and, eventually, help her become a permanent resident. It’s hard to put into words how much this has changed our lives.
If you’re in a situation where you need an immigration lawyer who will fight for you and show real compassion, I cannot recommend Michael Ibrahim enough. He turned what felt like an impossible situation into a success, and for that, my family will forever be grateful.

I wish I could give 0 stars. If you Hire Mike as your attorney be careful not to hurt his feelings or talk to him like he did something wrong. I hired mike over a year ago and he started calling and texting me weeks before my court date that he still needed additional information after we already met and discussed multiple times that he had everything. Everytime i talked to Mike, the story changed about how my case would look. I had a court case in December that Mike told me i can appear on zoom. Turns out he or his office made a mistake and that cost me 3 more months and a prosecutor that went so hard on me Mike did not prepare me or expect it himself. Because of this mistake he promised me either a partial refund or to fill out my N-400. I offcourse took the N-400 as i understand mistakes happen and did not at any point blame or even raise my voice at Mike. Me and my family were deeply affected and saddened by our case being dragged on another 3 months from what should have been an amazing christmas. Fast forward to this month. Our case is won (thank god, he was so unprepared even my criminal record chart had someone else’s name on it). He told me we can start filing my N-400 in a few weeks. I thought it was strange because he always told me we can do it right after the case is won. I was just so happy to have the case finally done after 5 years. Mike took weeks to start my case and didnt even reach out to me i had to be the only initiating everything. Aftwr a few weeks i got annoyed and told him how i feel like hes not taking my case serious and all he could tell me is “did we win or no?” Which was very heartbreaking to hear from a person who you forgave for making such a big mistake.  Never did i yell at him, call him names or even disrespect him. He told me he doesnt feel comfortable being my lawyer anymore and that he wont refund me or do anything to make things right. After this i was so stressed and me and my mother did not know what to do because we would never think Mike would go back on his word after what he did to us, and spend an hour on the phone with me “apologizing “man to man” and promising to make it right. Claiming it never happened before” even after all this i still apologized for criticizing him. (Yeah i know i cant believe it either) Clearly he took any chance he could to get out of doing what he promised. It was only after I persistently kept texting him that i will not forgive or forget that he started to take me more serious. He offered a phone call to try and get me to say the wrong things so he can find a way out. I have a 10 minute recording with Mike where he insults my intelligence because i am an immigrant he thought i did not know what the word “ethical” means and he asked asked me very rudely and laughing if i can even tell him what that means. He finally agreed to a refund because he knew he was not in the right obviously. The refund is definitely not worth what i went through and how unseriously mike took my case. I would only go to Mike with very easy cases that dont require a lot of communication or hard work. Would not recommend this person to my worst enemy.

“I can’t remember the last time someone hurled so many accusations at one time”
Well maybe you should do your job better Mike this is why people pay you thousands!!

You could have easily ruined my life and i forgave you but you couldnt stand to hear that.

Mike was texting me like a heartbroken girlfriend. He should definitely learn to control his feelings and keep them away from business!!
